response.justification: Candidate has 13+ years of solid software development experience and some adjacent tooling work (CI/CD setup, test frameworks in D, Selenium course creation), but the resume shows no formal people management or team leadership experience, which is the core requirement for this Engineering Manager role. There is also no mobile-platform ecosystem work at scale and no evidence of leading organizational change or managing engineers. Strong IC profile, but a clear mismatch for a Level II Manager position focused on engineering productivity at a mobile-platform scale.
strengths: 13+ years of polyglot software development across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Demonstrated developer-tooling work: test runner (Trial), assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD infrastructure, MCP code generators
Track record of teaching, workshops, and knowledge sharing (Selenium course, Refactoring Dojos, Web Internals workshop)
concerns: No explicit people-management or direct-report leadership experience listed — fails the 2-year people management minimum
No evidence of mobile-platform ecosystem work at the scale implied (iOS/Apple TV work was a small, one-off contribution a decade ago)
No experience leading organizational change or owning team roadmap/performance processes

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and testing infrastructure background
